speaker: interviewer
text: I'm just going to slate it, because it's a new tape.

speaker: interviewee
text: Is it ridiculous to put me there when the seat was empty?

speaker: interviewer
text: It's November the 20th, 1996. I'm conducting an interview with Miriam Freedman. This is tape number six. Would you like to tell us about this photograph, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. This was my class, that I remember. I must have been about five or six year old. I'm the fourth from the first row, on the left. You can see only half of my face.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was it taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: It was taken in Bratislava, in a school.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell us about this photograph, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. This is a building, [NON-ENGLISH], 42, I think. Where you see the arrow, that's the room that we were hiding the first part of-- in hiding.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you know when it was taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was taken-- I have taken this one about 10 years ago.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell us about this one, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. This is part of the cellar. This in a basement-- it was in a basement that is a cellar. And within the cellar, where the arrow is, is a hole carved out where we were hiding during emergency. Eight people. It's approximately 4 foot by 6.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell us about this one, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. This is Helenka Galova and William Gavalovic. This a part of the team who rescued us during the war. At that time, they were engaged. Helenka was very, very young, about 23 year old, and William was approximately 20 years older than her.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you know what year this picture was taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, when they got married, what is about 50 years ago. Approximately 50 years ago.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell us about this one, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. This is Helenka and William Gavalovic at their wedding day. On the right is my uncle Fisher-- Miklos Fisher. And on the left, I see, is a director of the mill.

speaker: interviewer
text: How was your uncle related to you?

speaker: interviewee
text: His wife was my mother's sister.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. Can you tell us about this one?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. This is auntie Seren on the left, and auntie Lilly. This was taken after the war. Two of these people were hiding with us during the war.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you know when this picture was taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: I reckon it looks-- in the '40s, I think. On the right is auntie Ella. She's the wife of uncle Miklos. And in the middle is Robby, the son of Lilly, who is on the left. All of us were in hiding during the Second World War together.

speaker: interviewer
text: When was this picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This must have been taken about 60 years ago.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you us about this one, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. After the war, I joined some youth movement. This looks like Hashomer Hatzair-- I can't tell, exactly. I'm the second from the left.

speaker: interviewer
text: In the front row?

speaker: interviewee
text: Pardon?

speaker: interviewer
text: In the front row?

speaker: interviewee
text: In the front row, yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell us about this one, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: This photograph was taken very soon after the war, when we reunited the whole family. On the left is my sister Gertie [INAUDIBLE]. In the middle is my mother, Olga. Edith on the right, and I'm in the middle, squatting. In our garden, where we lived, 29 [NON-ENGLISH]. This is my sister Barbara, Bracha. She's my elder sister who left for Palestine in 1939.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you know when this was taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: I think this picture must have been taken just afterwards.

speaker: interviewer
text: After the war.

speaker: interviewee
text: After the war. [INAUDIBLE]. OK.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell us about this one, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, this is our wedding day. It was in Haifa in 1959, 15th of December.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell us about this one, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. This is the house that, after the war, we lived in. Is [NON-ENGLISH] number 29. This is with my auntie Ella.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell us about this one, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: All right. This is Helenka after the war. Oh-- I went to visit Helenka in 1985. And this is Helenka, me, her two children, [INAUDIBLE] and the granddaughter. I think that I made a mistake with the date. I think this picture was taken, it was three or four years ago. These are my two children-- Martin Solomon Freedman, and Allison [? Naomi ?]. It was photographed about five years ago.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where?

speaker: interviewee
text: Near Chichester. This picture was taken the wedding of my mother first granddaughter. [? Miri ?] is on the right, Barbara in the middle, my mother, and her husband Eric, in kibbutz near David. It was taken about 20 years ago.
